$comment: c67 (D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL-SYMBOL-ADDRESS): a file-scope AGGREGATE global (array-of-struct, with a nested struct) whose initializer carries LINK-TIME-CONSTANT members — a function address (`seven`), a `&global` (`&target`), and string literals — the sqlite aSyscall[] shape. const-eval can't fold an address inside an aggregate, so it falls to runtime-init and trips the bit-field-rvalue fail-loud guard; c67 instead emits STATIC DATA with abs64 RELOCATIONS at the member offsets (extending the F5 scalar D-CSUBSET-SYMBOL-ADDRESS-GLOBAL mechanism to aggregate MEMBERS). The aggregate lands in writable-at-load .data; the linker patches the resolved (PIE-slid) VAs into the member slots. RUNTIME-verified: call the fn-ptr member, deref &target through the nested struct, read the rodata string bytes, and compare the trailing null fn-ptr == 0. exit 42 iff every member verifies — a wrong/unpatched reloc SIGSEGVs or yields a non-42 exit, never a silent pass. Red-on-disable: revert the c67 classify arm (tryClassifyAggregateConst/tryClassifyNullPointerConst/the Cast+Ref symbol-addr peels) OR the asm encodeAggregateValue reloc-leaf arm -> the table falls to runtime-init -> H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind (D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-RVALUE-RUNTIME) on every target. The release arm runs the shipped optimizer pipeline over the new aggregate data-item relocations.
